threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Overground instead of just 'the train'. Locals know it’s more than just a colour on the map.
Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 35, debating whether to go home or crash a wedding. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I heard a spontaneous jam session from my balcony, with people shouting ‘Berliner Luft’ across the street. That’s when I felt home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Schöneberg, you'll catch the faintest sound of spontaneous opera coming from the park on warm evenings—locals just belt it out, no stage needed.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to avoid 16th and Mission at 5 PM; it’s the real-life game of Frogger with those buses and bike lanes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo, top hat and all, riding the 14. He chatted with a lady in sweatpants about the best taco spots. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a party zone. They miss the quiet streets where locals still gossip over afternoon vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the bus routes—especially when they try to navigate without the TMB app.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'thank you' to the bus driver? Just a nod around here, mate. You’ll catch on soon enough.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and a guy argued with the driver about the 'real' Brixton Pie. He swore it’s a secret recipe, no one else knows.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I heard a spontaneous rap battle in the park, with kids gathering around like it was a festival. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know you’re in Kreuzberg when the kebab shop has a rotating art installation on the ceiling and serves homemade sauces you can't find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they cringe at the thought of a hipster taco truck lining up in front of La Taqueria.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 L last week packed tighter than a sardine tin, but the guy next to me was serenading the crowd with an oldie. That’s Muni life, eh?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think every bar here serves the same tapas, but the locals are fiercely loyal to their neighborhood favorites, each with secret recipes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confused expressions at the wild flow of traffic on Carrer verda. The locals glide through like it's a dance.
